fix: correct N/S wind comparator arg order in isWindwardEdge

N and S comparators were single-arg (y) => ... but called as
comparator(x, y), so x was received in the y slot. All cells at
y=0/y=19 with N/S wind were incorrectly getting zero wind damage.

perf: parallel tool call execution in ACTING state

reducing latency from N×tool_latency to max(tool_latency).

events for observability.

commit.message:
feat: heartbeat-driven sleep wake timer (increment 6)

When the loop enters SLEEPING state, compute the next time-based HEARTBEAT
entry (cron or ISO timestamp) and set a setTimeout to wake just-in-time.
This replaces polling — cron entries fire on schedule even while sleeping.

- Add nextCronOccurrence() and computeNextWakeTime() to HeartbeatParser
- Add SleepWakeTimer class (+ InMemorySleepWakeTimer for tests)
- Wire into LoopOrchestrator: arm on enterSleep/initializeSleeping, clear on wake
- Fix initializeSleeping() to pause watchdog (known bug from code review)
- Document sleep-aware wake timer in README and HEARTBEAT template

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: wire real Azure integrations — close 6 integration gaps

Storage Mode Factory (#34):
- Central factory (infrastructure/factory.py) switches memory/azure based on config
- storage_mode='memory' → InMemory repos (default, for dev/tests)
- storage_mode='azure' → SQL repos with DatabaseAdapter

SQL Repositories (#25):
- SqlSubmissionRepository, SqlPolicyRepository, SqlClaimRepository, SqlComplianceRepository
- All use parameterized queries against deployed Azure SQL schema
- All API endpoints now use factory-provided repos instead of hardcoded InMemory

Foundry Agent Integration (#30):
- FoundryAgentClient calls deployed agents via Responses API with agent_reference
- InsuranceAgent.execute_with_foundry() tries Foundry first, falls back to local
- Orchestrator chains Foundry calls for multi-agent workflows
- New POST /submissions/{id}/process endpoint for full Foundry workflow

Compliance Persistence (#31):
- SqlComplianceRepository for decision_records and audit_events tables
- InMemoryComplianceRepository with matching interface for dev mode
- Both wired via factory

Dashboard Real API (#33):
- USE_MOCK configurable via VITE_USE_MOCK env var
- Production build (.env.production) uses real API
- All API calls wrapped in try/catch with mock fallback
- Paginated response handling

Quality: 275 tests passing, ruff clean, bandit clean, dashboard builds.

commit.message:
feat: HA OAuth login for MA addon mode

When Music Assistant runs as HA addon, username/password login is
unavailable. Add /api/ma/ha-login endpoint that implements the full
HA OAuth flow programmatically:

1. Get OAuth state from MA /auth/authorize
2. Run HA login_flow with user credentials
3. Handle MFA/TOTP step if 2FA is enabled
4. Exchange HA auth code via MA /auth/callback
5. Extract and save MA token to config.json

Frontend: replace static addon hint with interactive HA login form
including TOTP input for 2FA. The form appears automatically when
MA addon mode is detected via Discover.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Purge dashboard entries older than 14 days (#304)

* Purge dashboard entries older than 14 days

When updating plugin JSON files, filter out Quality and Efficiency
entries whose date is older than 14 days. If all entries are purged,
delete the JSON file so it is excluded from the components.json
manifest and no longer appears in the dashboard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move stale file cleanup to workflow level

Address review feedback: the delete-if-empty branch in the script was
unreachable because fresh entries are always appended before purging.
Instead, add a workflow step that scans all existing data files after
per-plugin generation and removes any whose entries are entirely older
than 14 days.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move stale file purge logic into the script

Add a -PurgeStaleFiles/-DataDir parameter set so the workflow calls
the script instead of duplicating the retention logic inline. The
retention window is defined once in the script.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make retention days configurable via workflow env var

Add -RetentionDays parameter to the script (mandatory for
-PurgeStaleFiles, optional for generate mode) and define
DASHBOARD_RETENTION_DAYS env var at the workflow level so the value
is controlled in one place.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: fix comment and add entries guard

- Fix workflow comment to accurately describe the purge behavior
- Guard against missing 'entries' key in purge mode to avoid silently
  skipping malformed plugin JSON files

* Update eng/dashboard/generate-benchmark-data.ps1

commit.message:
fix: guard against null IDENTIFIER tokens from ANTLR4 error recovery

ANTLR4 error recovery can produce declaration nodes with null IDENTIFIER
terminal tokens (e.g. when a function parameter list has a trailing comma
before the closing paren). SemanticAnalyzer now null-guards all such
dereferences to prevent NullReferenceException (CS8785) in the source
generator when processing scripts with commented-out parameters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove Send from Entry/LruEntry, relax Sync bounds (#34)

* Initial plan

* Remove Send impl from Entry/LruEntry, relax Sync bounds, bump version to 0.2.1

Entry and LruEntry are RAII lock guards that should not be Send (similar to
MutexGuard). The Sync impl only needs K: Sync + V: Sync since sharing &Entry
only involves shared references, not ownership transfer.

Co-authored-by: SF-Zhou <7477599+SF-Zhou@users.noreply.github.com>
